_comment_kinds: Allowed kind values: skill, agent, cargo-test, script, manual, doc-section.
_comment_script: kind:script 'command' must contain exactly ONE scripts/* path token.
_comment_manual: kind:manual entries MUST include a non-empty 'steps' array.

AC-001-a.notes: Reviewer confirms five new change_caption_<field>_setting functions exist in src-tauri/src/commands/app_settings.rs and are registered in src-tauri/src/lib.rs invoke_handler + specta collect_commands blocks.

AC-001-b.notes: Round-trip + clamp unit tests in app_settings.rs test module exercise each of the five keys at their range edges and assert the clamped value persists.

AC-001-c.notes: Same test module as AC-001-b; each of the five keys has a write-then-read assertion via settings::get_settings.

AC-002-a.notes: Reviewer confirms the five settingUpdaters entries exist in src/stores/settingsStore.ts and invoke the specta-generated commands.

steps: Change each of: font family dropdown, radius slider, padding-x slider, padding-y slider, max-width-percent slider
Confirm NO 'No handler for setting: caption_...' warnings appear in the console
Close and relaunch the app; confirm each of the five values persisted
After cargo tauri dev debug build regenerates src/bindings.ts
Confirm all five command names appear in the file

AC-003-a.notes: Fixture-load test asserts a pre-bundle settings.json (with and without each caption_* key) deserializes without error and fields equal on-disk or default values.

AC-003-b.notes: Reviewer diffs src-tauri/src/settings/types.rs for this bundle and confirms no changes to caption_* field declarations.

AC-004-a.notes: cargo check via --no-run builds all tests without executing; used as the static Rust gate.

AC-004-b.notes: Wrapper runs `npm run lint` and reports exit 0. Stub at scripts/lint-and-build-gate.ps1 until implementation lands (exits 2 = not-implemented).

AC-004-c.notes: i18n parity trivially maintained; no key changes in this bundle.

AC-004-d.notes: Passes unchanged if app_settings.rs stays under 800 lines; on trip, the blueprint-prescribed submodule split runs and the gate passes post-split.
